channel.title: HoopsHype Rumors - Detroit Pistons
channel.description: Detroit Pistons trade rumors and free agency buzz from HoopsHype

item.title: Duren: “I’ve just gotta be better, man. I have no …
item.description: ‪ ‪Omari Sankofa II‬: Duren: “I’ve just gotta be better, man. I have no excuses.”

item.title: JB Bickerstaff points out Duncan Robinson was being …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: JB Bickerstaff points out Duncan Robinson was being held coming off screens. “We’ll go back, we’ll watch it, we’ll send the clips in to the league, we’ll see what it is. But we have to play better.”

item.title: Game 4 vs. the Cavs was the fifth time Jalen Duren's …
item.description: ESPN Insights: Game 4 vs. the Cavs was the fifth time Jalen Duren's been held to single-digit points in the playoffs. He was held under 10 points in just six of his 70 games in the regular season.

item.title: Donovan Mitchell demolishes Pistons in the second half to even series
item.description: Danny Cunningham: #Cavs beat the Pistons 112-103 in a game that wasn't as close as the final score. Donovan Mitchell had one of his best games as a member of the Cavs, finishing with 43 points -- 39 of them in the 2nd half. Harden had 24 and hit five 3s. Series is tied at 2-2.

item.title: Game 4 vs. Detroit marks Donovan Mitchell's fourth …
item.description: ESPN Insight: Game 4 vs. Detroit marks Donovan Mitchell's fourth 40-point playoff game with the Cavaliers, passing Kyrie Irving for second-most in franchise history . Only LeBron James (24) has more.

item.title: JB Bickerstaff on what’s enabled Tobias Harris to …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: JB Bickerstaff on what’s enabled Tobias Harris to score 20+ in eight straight games: “His want to help this team and it’s as simple as that. He sees where a need is and he has the ability to fill that need.”

item.title: Caris LeVert is available, Kevin Huerter remains OUT
item.description: Jacob Richman: Caris LeVert is available, Kevin Huerter remains OUT.

item.title: The Cavaliers have listed Thomas Bryant (low back …
item.description: The Cavaliers have listed Thomas Bryant (low back spasms) as questionable for Monday's Game 4 against Detroit.

item.title: NBA Last Two Minute Report from Game 3 shows 0 errors. …
item.description: Chris Fedor: NBA Last Two Minute Report from Game 3 shows 0 errors. Tobias Harris’ late contest on James Harden’s 3-point dagger was clean, not considered a foul. “Harris jumps to contest Harden's shot, but would otherwise avoid making contact if not for Harden extending forward.”

item.title: Caris LeVert listed as questionable for Game 4 vs. Cavs
item.description: The Pistons have downgraded Caris LeVert (right heel contusion) to questionable for Monday’s Game 4 against Cleveland. Kevin Huerter (left adductor strain) is also questionable.

item.title: Duren: “I’ve just gotta be better, man. I have no …
item.description: ‪ ‪Omari Sankofa II‬: Duren: “I’ve just gotta be better, man. I have no excuses.”

item.title: JB Bickerstaff points out Duncan Robinson was being …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: JB Bickerstaff points out Duncan Robinson was being held coming off screens. “We’ll go back, we’ll watch it, we’ll send the clips in to the league, we’ll see what it is. But we have to play better.”

item.title: Game 4 vs. the Cavs was the fifth time Jalen Duren's …
item.description: ESPN Insights: Game 4 vs. the Cavs was the fifth time Jalen Duren's been held to single-digit points in the playoffs. He was held under 10 points in just six of his 70 games in the regular season.

item.title: Donovan Mitchell demolishes Pistons in the second half to even series
item.description: Danny Cunningham: #Cavs beat the Pistons 112-103 in a game that wasn't as close as the final score. Donovan Mitchell had one of his best games as a member of the Cavs, finishing with 43 points -- 39 of them in the 2nd half. Harden had 24 and hit five 3s. Series is tied at 2-2.

item.title: Game 4 vs. Detroit marks Donovan Mitchell's fourth …
item.description: ESPN Insight: Game 4 vs. Detroit marks Donovan Mitchell's fourth 40-point playoff game with the Cavaliers, passing Kyrie Irving for second-most in franchise history . Only LeBron James (24) has more.

item.title: JB Bickerstaff on what’s enabled Tobias Harris to …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: JB Bickerstaff on what’s enabled Tobias Harris to score 20+ in eight straight games: “His want to help this team and it’s as simple as that. He sees where a need is and he has the ability to fill that need.”

item.title: Caris LeVert is available, Kevin Huerter remains OUT
item.description: Jacob Richman: Caris LeVert is available, Kevin Huerter remains OUT.

item.title: The Cavaliers have listed Thomas Bryant (low back …
item.description: The Cavaliers have listed Thomas Bryant (low back spasms) as questionable for Monday's Game 4 against Detroit.

item.title: NBA Last Two Minute Report from Game 3 shows 0 errors. …
item.description: Chris Fedor: NBA Last Two Minute Report from Game 3 shows 0 errors. Tobias Harris’ late contest on James Harden’s 3-point dagger was clean, not considered a foul. “Harris jumps to contest Harden's shot, but would otherwise avoid making contact if not for Harden extending forward.”

item.title: Caris LeVert listed as questionable for Game 4 vs. Cavs
item.description: The Pistons have downgraded Caris LeVert (right heel contusion) to questionable for Monday’s Game 4 against Cleveland. Kevin Huerter (left adductor strain) is also questionable.

item.title: Duncan Robinson and Caris LeVert listed as questionable for Game 5
item.description: The Pistons have downgraded Caris LeVert (right heel contusion) and Duncan Robinson (low back soreness) as questionable for Wednesday’s Game 5 against Cleveland. Kevin Huerter (left adductor strain) is also questionable.

item.title: “If Grant Hill was 100% healthy, he would've never …
item.description: Run It Back: “If Grant Hill was 100% healthy, he would've never left Detroit." Tim Hardaway Sr. says Grant Hill would've been "up there" if injuries didn't derail his career

item.title: Scripps Sports has scored local rights to its first …
item.description: Scripps Sports has scored local rights to its first NBA team, as the Pistons will air games over-the-air with the company beginning with the 2026-27 season. Terms of the deal were not disclosed. Games will air in the Detroit DMA, surrounding areas and across the state of Michigan. The move to Scripps comes after the team had long been on FanDuel Sports Network Detroit, which folded following the season. This will mark the first time local Pistons games will primarily be on free TV since 2005.

item.title: Sources told SBJ that the Hornets were among the teams …
item.description: Sources told SBJ that the Hornets were among the teams considering a deal with streaming platform Victory+. Sources also told SBJ last month that Fubo makes surprise pitch to all 13 NBA teams that fled Main Street. DAZN, Gray TV and Nexstar have also been reported as local options for teams. The Pistons’ former RSN also was the home to the Tigers and Red Wings, but those clubs – both owned by the Ilitch family -- launched Detroit SportsNet in collaboration with MLB Media. That first-of-its-kind,

item.title: Starting next season, the Pistons will air their …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: BREAKING: Starting next season, the Pistons will air their preseason and regular season games for free locally on Channel 20

item.title: “They’re getting more calls than we are.”
item.description: Hunter Patterson: J.B. when asked how the whistle has changed after Kenny Atkinson’s comments: “They’re getting more calls than we are.”

item.title: “Depending on the feel of the game we give guys those …
item.description: Hunter Patterson: J.B. on Isaiah Stewart’s limited minutes: “Depending on the feel of the game we give guys those opportunities based on what their role is for us. Stew’s done what we’ve asked him to do. It’s just seemed like in those games we’ve needed a spark and P Reed has that spark ability.”

item.title: “Stew’s done what we’ve asked him to do. It just …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: JB denied that Stew is dealing with a physical issue. On his minutes decrease: “Stew’s done what we’ve asked him to do. It just seemed like in those games we needed a spark and P Reed has that spark ability.”

item.title: “We lost guys a few times. Watching the ball in their …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: JB on their defensive mistakes the last two games: “We lost guys a few times. Watching the ball in their isos and they have guys who are really good relocation shooters … we have some things to improve.”

item.title: Duncan Robinson (low back soreness) out for Game 5 against Cavaliers
item.description: Jamal Collier: Duncan Robinson (low back soreness) is OUT tonight for Detroit. Kevin Huerter is active tonight for the first time this series

item.title: Cade Cunningham, Daniss Jenkins, Ausar Thompson, …
item.description: Hunter Patterson: Pistons starters: Cade Cunningham, Daniss Jenkins, Ausar Thompson, Tobias Harris, Jalen Duren

item.title: “With Cade, you cannot give him the same look. He’s …
item.description: Hunter Patterson: Kenny Atkinson on defending Cade: “With Cade, you cannot give him the same look. He’s like a great quarterback, you give the same look to him he’s gonna figure it out. Keep mixing and matching different coverages, different matchups.

item.title: James Harden goes off for 30 points, eight rebounds, six assists as Cavaliers win thrilling Game 5 in overtime over Detroit
item.description: StatMuse: James Harden in Game 5 vs Detroit: 30 PTS, 8 REB, 6 AST, 3 BLK. Joins SGA and Kobe as the only guards with those numbers in a playoff game since 2010.

item.title: Cade Cunningham had a game-high 39 points on 6/10 from …
item.description: Hunter Patterson: Cavs hold on to beat the Pistons 117-113 after outscoring them 14-10 in overtime. Cleveland takes a 3-2 series lead. Cade Cunningham had a game-high 39 points on 6/10 from distance, 9 assists, 7 boards and 2 steals.

item.title: Ties Isiah Thomas for the most 30 PT games by a Piston …
item.description: StatMuse: Cade Cunningham in Game 5 vs Cleveland: 39 PTS 7 REB 9 AST 2 STL Ties Isiah Thomas for the most 30 PT games by a Piston in a single playoff run.

item.title: Pistons led by 9 with 2:37 left in the 4th quarter
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Pistons led by 9 with 2:37 left in the 4th quarter. They were outscored 23-10 the through the end of the 4th & OT, shooting 4-for-17 from the field.

item.title: “You call that call anytime of the game. That call has …
item.description: NBA Courtside: P.J. Tucker and Brian Windhorst reach to the no call on Ausar Thompson last night: “You call that call anytime of the game. That call has to be made. 1000%. There’s no way you don’t make that call — I believe it was a foul. If I were a Pistons fan I wouldn’t listen to anything else”

item.title: Cade Cunningham: 'We all saw the play, I think it’s pretty clear it was a foul'
item.description: Hunter Patterson: Cade Cunningham on the final play of regulation: “We all saw the play, I think it’s pretty clear it was a foul. (Ausar) has the ball in his possession, he’s running forward, he gets tripped up. It’s a foul, it’s been a foul the whole rest of the game. Wasn’t a foul that time.”

item.title: On the late no-call against Ausar: “He fouled Ausar. …
item.description: ?Omari Sankofa II?: JB: “They started doubling and getting the ball out of Cade’s hands. We’ll find ways to fix it. We’ll be better.” On the late no-call against Ausar: “He fouled Ausar. It’s clear. He trips him when he’s going for a loose ball. End of game situation, that’s tough.”

item.title: Cade Cunningham on the mindset moving forward: “It’s …
item.description: Hunter Patterson: Cade Cunningham on the mindset moving forward: “It’s first to four wins. We know that, we’ve had our back against the wall before. At the end of the day if we can’t win a game on the road, how far are we really going to get in the playoffs? … Got to go win a game on the road.”

item.title: “It’s about exploiting whatever they’re doing that’s …
item.description: Hunter Patterson: Asked Cade Cunningham about the challenge of generating quality offense outside of him: “It’s about exploiting whatever they’re doing that’s giving us advantages. … I just haven’t done a good enough job of drawing the two and then getting the ball to guys cleanly.”

item.title: Cleveland outscored Detroit 23-10, shot 50% to the …
item.description: ?Kurt Helin?: In the final 3 minutes and OT, Cleveland outscored Detroit 23-10, shot 50% to the Pistons 23.5%, was 2-of-3 from 3 while Detroit was 0-of-5, and Mitchell was 4-of-5 with 9 points, while Cunningham was 1-of-4 for 2 points.

item.title: “Just sticking with it. Every single minute, every …
item.description: Evan Mobley emphasized composure and collective execution after the Cleveland Cavaliers escaped with a 117-113 overtime win over the Detroit Pistons in Game 5 of the Eastern Conference semifinals. “Just sticking with it. Every single minute, every single second counts at the end of games like this and I think we stuck with it till the very end,” Mobley said, describing the mindset behind Cleveland’s comeback from a nine-point deficit in the final minutes of regulation. The Cavaliers turned a 103

item.title: Pistons got Evan Mobley looking like Scar
item.description: ?Spencer Davies?: Pistons got Evan Mobley looking like Scar.

item.title: Max Strus: "I look better? You're saying I look …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Max Strus: "I look better? You're saying I look bigger?" "I do. I think you look like the Pistons. They got a bunch of big guys. I think you look like them" Max: "I'll take that as a compliment" ??

item.title: Jalen Duren: 'I feel I can be better in all aspects of the game'
item.description: ?Omari Sankofa II?: Duren: “Absolutely. I feel I can be better in all aspects of the game, and continue to grind and continue to grow.” On Reed closing the 4th and OT: “My brother handled it. B-Ball came in ready to go and did his thing.”

item.title: Pistons upgrade Duncan Robinson to questionable for Game 6
item.description: The Pistons have upgraded Duncan Robinson (low back soreness) to questionable for Friday's Game 6 against Cleveland. Kevin Huerter (left adductor strain) and Caris LeVert (right heel contusion) are also questionable.
